title: Opening Range Breakout Tool
subtitle: 3-Stage Breakout Confirmation — Break → Retest → Re-break with ATR SL/TP
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: MEDIUM EXTRACT — 3-stage breakout confirmation pattern (break → retest → re-break) is a useful false-breakout filter. Opening range concept maps to our session-based scanning window. ATR×1.5 SL validates our floor.

core_concept.opening_range: High/Low of first RangeMinutes (default 15) after session open, OR high/low of last-bar-of-prior-session + first-bar-of-current-session.

three_stage_breakout.stage_1: Price closes beyond range boundary (initial break)
three_stage_breakout.stage_2: Price returns and touches the boundary (retest)
three_stage_breakout.stage_3: Price closes beyond boundary again (re-break) → ENTRY

core_concept.purpose_of_3_stages: Filters false breakouts — requires genuine momentum (twice), not just a spike
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Our zone interaction + departure candle is a 2-stage check (touch zone, close away). Adding a retest requirement makes it 3-stage, more selective. Opening range as a session-based zone is another zone type we could add.

sl_tp.sl: ATR * 1.5 from entry (validates our min_sl = 2*ATR floor — 1.5 is slightly more aggressive)
sl_tp.tp: ATR * 1.5 (1:1 RR) — too low for our min 1.5 RRR

keep: 3-stage breakout confirmation (break → retest → re-break) as false-breakout filter
Opening range as a session-zone type (high/low of first 15min = key reference levels)
ATR×1.5 SL as a validated industry setting
discard: Opening range is a specific strategy — not our primary approach
improve: Apply 3-stage logic to zone retests: first touch → pullback → second touch = higher confidence entry
Add session opening range as a supplemental zone type in ZoneIdentifier
